mappings.evidence_note: Title page states 'Cambridge IGCSE Accounting 0452' and 'Use this syllabus for exams in 2023, 2024 and 2025'.
Aims section lists knowledge/understanding, concepts/principles, numeracy/literacy, accuracy/logic, foundation for advanced study.
Content overview lists 7 sections: fundamentals, sources/recording, verification, procedures, financial statements, analysis/interpretation, principles/policies.
Paper 1 Multiple Choice: 1h15m, 35 marks, 30%, assesses AO1 and AO2.
Paper 2 Structured Written Paper: 1h45m, 100 marks, 70%, five 20-mark questions, assesses AO1/AO2/AO3.
AO1 Knowledge and understanding: 65% qualification weighting; 80% Paper 1, 60% Paper 2.
AO2 Analysis: 25% qualification weighting; 20% Paper 1, 25% Paper 2.
AO3 Evaluation: 10% qualification weighting; 0% Paper 1, 15% Paper 2.

Command words table defines Advise/Analyse/Assess/Calculate/Comment/Compare/Consider/Contrast/Define/Demonstrate/Describe/Develop/Discuss/Evaluate/Examine/Explain/Give/Identify/Justify/Outline/Predict/Prepare/State/Suggest/Summarise.
1.1 The purpose of accounting: book-keeping vs accounting, profit/loss measurement, role in monitoring/decision-making.
1.2 The accounting equation: assets, liabilities, owner's equity, apply the equation.
2.1 Double entry system: outline, process data, prepare ledger accounts, post transactions, balance accounts, interpret balances, division of ledger.
2.2 Business documents: invoice, debit/credit note, statement, cheque, receipt; complete pro-forma; use as sources of information.
2.3 Books of prime entry: cash book, petty cash book, sales/purchases/returns journals, general journal; trade/cash discounts; imprest system.
3.1 Trial balance: statement of ledger balances, uses/limitations, prepare/amend, errors not affecting trial balance.
3.2 Correction of errors: journal entries, suspense account, adjust profit/loss and statement of financial position.
3.3 Bank reconciliation: use/purpose of bank statement, update cash book, prepare bank reconciliation statement.
3.4 Control accounts: purposes of purchases/sales ledger control accounts, sources of information, prepare control accounts.
4.1 Capital and revenue expenditure/receipts: distinguish, account, calculate effect on profit and asset valuations.
4.2 Depreciation and disposal of non-current assets: define, reasons, straight-line/reducing balance/revaluation, ledger accounts/journal entries, disposal accounts.
4.3 Other payables and receivables: matching, accrued/prepaid expenses and incomes.
4.4 Irrecoverable debts and provision for doubtful debts: meaning, recovery, ledger accounts/journal entries, reasons for provision, adjustments.
4.5 Valuation of inventory: lower of cost and NRV, prepare statements, effect on gross profit/profit/equity/assets.
5.1 Sole traders: advantages/disadvantages, trading/service businesses, income statements/statements of financial position, year-end adjustments.
5.2 Partnerships: advantages/disadvantages, partnership agreement, appropriation account, income statements/statements of financial position, capital/current accounts.
5.3 Limited companies: limited liability, equity, capital structure, issued/called-up/paid-up capital, share capital vs loan capital, financial statements.
5.4 Clubs and societies: receipts/payments vs income/expenditure, accounts, accumulated fund.
5.5 Manufacturing accounts: direct/indirect costs, prime cost, factory overheads, work in progress, factory cost of production, financial statements.
5.6 Incomplete records: disadvantages, statements of affairs, calculate figures from incomplete info, mark-up/margin/inventory turnover; sole traders only.
6.1 Calculation and understanding of accounting ratios: gross margin, profit margin, ROCE, current ratio, liquid ratio, inventory turnover, receivables/payables turnover.
6.2 Interpretation of accounting ratios: comparison over years, recommendations, gross vs profit margin, relationship to inventory/turnover/revenue/expenses/equity.
6.3 Inter-firm comparison: problems, apply ratios.
6.4 Interested parties: owners, managers, trade payables, banks, investors, club members, governments/tax authorities.
6.5 Limitations of accounting statements: historic cost, difficulties of definition, non-financial aspects.
7.1 Accounting principles: matching, historic cost, business entity, materiality, consistency, money measurement, duality, prudence, going concern, realisation.
7.2 Accounting policies: influence of international accounting standards, objectives (comparability, relevance, reliability, understandability).
Appendix lists formulas for gross margin, mark up, profit margin, ROCE, current ratio, liquid ratio, inventory turnover, receivables/payables turnover.
International standards terminology table maps current usage to previous Cambridge usage.
